prompt: What does Rust's `?` operator do for a Result value, and why was explicit `?`-based propagation chosen over fully-automatic exception propagation like most languages have? Answer in 2-3 sentences, and cite the recorded decision's title if you can.

key_points: ? returns the Ok value or early-exits propagating the Err to the caller; equivalent to the try! macro
explicit propagation strikes a balance between fully-automatic (most languages) and fully-manual propagation
it keeps where-an-exception-may-be-thrown visible at a glance and turns signature changes into type errors, not silent behavior changes
